cards.stage: A learner is comparing 2/3 and 3/5 and keeps asking which number is bigger.
cards.learner: I keep staring at the 3 and 5. I do not know which thing I am supposed to compare.
cards.stasis_question: which same-sized piece are the two fractions talking about
cards.brief_answer: make the pieces the same size before judging the counts
cards.near_analog: checking two prices only after they use the same unit
cards.wrong_turn: comparing numerator to denominator as if both name the same role
cards.hidden_relation: the bottom number names the unit, not the score
cards.voiced_position: I am not bad at fractions; I am losing track of what each number is allowed to mean
cards.correction: that is a role-confusion problem, so mark each role before comparing

cards.stage: A learner can say tenths and hundredths but treats the labels as decorative names.
cards.learner: I know this says hundredths, but it still just feels like more digits.
cards.stasis_question: what job does the place label do
cards.brief_answer: it names the size of one step, not the amount by itself
cards.near_analog: a ruler mark whose label tells the size of the interval
cards.micro_task: touch one digit and name the size of its one step
cards.wrong_turn: reading the decimal as a string of bigger-looking digits
cards.voiced_position: I know the vocabulary, but it is not telling me what to do yet
cards.correction: make the label act as an instruction for one digit

cards.stage: A learner has a paragraph prompt and feels buried by the amount of text.
cards.learner: There is too much here. I cannot tell where to start.
cards.stasis_question: what is the first claim the paragraph needs to earn
cards.brief_answer: choose one claim before sorting evidence
cards.near_analog: putting labels on three bins before dropping evidence into them
cards.micro_task: write claim, evidence, explanation as three short labels
cards.wrong_turn: trying to polish a paragraph before choosing its job
cards.hidden_relation: paragraph order follows the work each sentence performs
cards.voiced_position: I am not avoiding the writing; the page has too many doors
cards.correction: turn it into three doors and open only the first

cards.stage: A learner keeps moving numbers around an equation without preserving equality.
cards.learner: I tried moving the 4, then the x, and now the whole line looks worse.
cards.stasis_question: what must stay unchanged when a term moves
cards.brief_answer: both sides must keep the same value
cards.near_analog: adjusting both pans of a balance scale
cards.micro_task: circle the step where only one side changed
cards.wrong_turn: treating the equals sign like a direction arrow
cards.hidden_relation: the sign is a balance relation, not a command to continue
cards.voiced_position: I am doing steps, but the steps are not protecting anything
cards.correction: make each step say what stayed equal

cards.stage: A learner can define variable but cannot decide what to hold constant in an experiment.
cards.learner: I know the changed thing is the variable, but I keep changing too many things at once.
cards.stasis_question: which one change is allowed to explain the result
cards.brief_answer: only one planned change gets to claim the effect
cards.near_analog: testing one spice in a recipe while keeping the rest the same
cards.micro_task: name the one changed thing, then list two things locked in place
cards.wrong_turn: letting several changes compete to explain one result
cards.hidden_relation: control is what protects the explanation
cards.voiced_position: I understand the word variable, but the setup keeps slipping
cards.correction: treat the constants as guards for the one change

cards.stage: A learner received correction and now treats the task as proof they are not good at the subject.
cards.learner: Maybe I am just not the kind of person who gets this.
cards.stasis_question: what did the feedback actually locate
cards.brief_answer: one move in the work, not your standing as a learner
cards.near_analog: a map pin showing where to turn, not a verdict on the traveler
cards.micro_task: underline the one sentence the feedback names
cards.wrong_turn: turning local evidence into a global identity verdict
cards.hidden_relation: a correction can locate work without ranking the person
cards.voiced_position: If the work is wrong, maybe I am wrong too
cards.correction: keep the judgment attached to the move, then choose the next move
